title: Peshk Vri'syk
content:

{{Quote|The Bothans want a pilot in Rogue Squadron because ''they'' found the second Death Star and ''we'' killed it.|Wedge Antilles|<ref name="X-Wing: Rogue Squadron" />}}
'''Peshk Vri'syk''' was a [[Gender/Legends|male]] [[Bothan/Legends|Bothan]] [[Pilot/Legends|pilot]] who served in [[Rogue Squadron/Legends|Rogue Squadron]], the [[New Republic/Legends|New Republic's]] elite [[Starfighter/Legends|starfighter]] unit, in [[6 ABY/Legends|6 ABY]]. [[Reproduction/Legends|Born]] on [[Bothawui/Legends|Bothawui]], Vri'syk was trained in [[Starfighter combat/Legends|flight combat]] at the [[Bothan Martial Academy]]. His performance earned him an appointment to Rogue Squadron when the unit was reconstituted in 6 ABY by [[Commander/Legends|Commander]] [[Wedge Antilles/Legends|Wedge Antilles]]. Vri'syk's assignment was also [[Politics/Legends|politically]] motivated; the Bothans wanted a member of their [[Species/Legends|species]] represented in the New Republic's flagship [[Squadron/Legends|squadron]] because their [[Bothan Spynet/Legends|spies]] had [[Raid on the Suprosa|uncovered]] the location of the [[Death Star II/Legends|second Death Star]], which Rogue Squadron subsequently assisted in [[Battle of Endor/Legends|destroying]]. The New Republic assented to the request.

Although inexperienced, Vri'syk was an excellent pilot, and his training scores—along with the rest of the squadron's new recruits—were better than those of many front-line combat units. Vri'syk saw [[Battle in the Chorax system|action]] at [[Chorax]], and served in the [[Rout of Hensara|effort]] to extract New Republic assets from the planet [[Hensara III]]. He [[Raid on Talasea|survived]] a covert [[Stormtrooper/Legends|stormtrooper]] attack against the Rogue Squadron base on the planet [[Talasea]], and participated in a [[Defense of Vladet|retaliatory strike]] against [[Admiral/Legends|Admiral]] [[Devlia]], the [[Imperial officer/Legends|Imperial officer]] who had ordered the assault. Vri'syk was [[Death/Legends|killed]] during the [[First Battle of Borleias]] when a heavy [[Ion cannon/Legends|ion cannon]] blast destroyed his [[T-65 X-wing starfighter/Legends|X-wing]] starfighter. His legacy was later exaggerated by the Bothans to advance their political achievements.

==Biography==
===Early life===
{{Quote|I graduated a year behind Peshk Vri'syk. He was good enough to join Rogue Squadron last year, and I was his equal when we were trained.|Asyr Sei'lar|<ref name="Wedge's Gamble">''[[X-Wing: Wedge's Gamble]]''</ref>}}
[[File:Bothawui_NEGAS.jpg|thumb|left|180px|Bothawui, Peshk Vri'syk's home planet.]]
Peshk Vri'syk was a [[Gender/Legends|male]] [[Bothan/Legends|Bothan]] [[Reproduction/Legends|born]] on the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Bothawui/Legends|Bothawui]].<ref name="X-Wing: Rogue Squadron" /> Educated at the [[Bothan Martial Academy]], Vri'syk was trained in [[Starfighter/Legends|starfighter]] operations and [[Starfighter combat/Legends|combat]]. He was a [[Standard year/Legends|year]] ahead of the female, [[Asyr Sei'lar]], and the two were considered to be equally skilled during their training.<ref name="Wedge's Gamble" /> In [[6 ABY/Legends|6 ABY]], the [[New Republic/Legends|New Republic's]] elite starfighter unit, [[Rogue Squadron/Legends|Rogue Squadron]], was in the process of being reconstituted by [[Commander/Legends|Commander]] [[Wedge Antilles/Legends|Wedge Antilles]]. Vri'syk's skills earned him a posting to the [[Squadron/Legends|squadron]], although his assignment was partly spurred by [[Politics/Legends|political]] motivations. The Bothans wanted their [[Species/Legends|species]] represented in the New Republic's foremost starfighter squadron as Bothan [[Spy/Legends|spies]] had been responsible for locating the [[Death Star II/Legends|second]] [[Death Star/Legends|Death Star]], which Rogue Squadron had subsequently taken a role in destroying during the [[Battle of Endor/Legends|Battle of Endor]]. The New Republic agreed to the request as a way of showing the Bothans that their contributions were valued.<ref name="X-Wing: Rogue Squadron" />

Although the political pressure benefited Vri'syk, Antilles assured [[Admiral/Legends|Admiral]] [[Gial Ackbar/Legends|Gial Ackbar]], the [[Supreme Commander (Alliance)/Legends|Supreme Commander]] of the [[New Republic Defense Force/Legends|New Republic Defense Force]], that none of the [[Pilot/Legends|pilots]] selected for political reasons—which included the [[Thyferra/Legends|Thyferrans]] [[Bror Jace]] and [[Erisi Dlarit]] to appease the competing [[Bacta Cartel/Legends|Bacta Cartels]] on their [[Homeworld/Legends|homeworld]], Thyferra—was inferior to any other candidates. Along with the rest of the newly formed squadron, Vri'syk was posted to the [[Folor Base|training base]] on [[Folor]], a [[Moon/Legends|moon]] of the planet [[Commenor/Legends|Commenor]], where he was given the [[Callsign/Legends|call sign]] [[Rogue Two/Legends|Rogue Two]], a placement in [[One Flight]], and assigned as Antilles' [[Wingmate/Legends|wingman]].<ref name="X-Wing: Rogue Squadron" />

===Rogue Squadron===
{{Quote|The worlds who want to join us have; those who don't, haven't; and those who aren't sure will need some convincing. Thyferra, for example, is the source of ninety-five percent of the bacta in the galaxy. They're neutral right now, and making grand profits selling to all sides, but we want them in our camp. Putting two of their people in Rogue Squadron sends a message to the Thyferrans that we value them. The same goes for having the Bothan in the squadron.|Corran Horn|<ref name="X-Wing: Rogue Squadron" />}}
Alongside the rest of the squadron, Vri'syk participated in training exercises while stationed on Folor as Antilles sought to mold his new recruits into a viable squadron. During an exercise on the base's [[Trench (Folor)|gunnery range]], Antilles ordered one of the Rogues, the [[Corellian/Legends|Corellian]] [[Corran Horn/Legends|Corran Horn]], to make the first run and complete a series of flying and targeting challenges. The Corellian complied, and aimed to score a target that the other members of the squadron would not be able to match in an effort to impress the Commander. However, Antilles felt that Horn needed to learn a lesson in humility and teamwork, and ordered Horn's [[Astromech droid/Legends|astromech droid]], [[Whistler]], to transmit the pilot's [[Sensor/Legends|sensor]] data to the rest of the squadron so that they would benefit from Horn's run. Horn subsequently scored 3250 out of a possible 5000, and was ordered back to base with no knowledge that the rest of the squadron would be using his data. Vri'syk tackled the exercise using Horn's data as ordered, scoring 4200. Antilles then privately revealed to Vri'syk what he had done to Horn and ordered the Bothan not to mention it to Horn, but to simply inform the Corellian of his score. Upon returning to base, the Bothan took pleasure in reporting his success to Horn, who had no idea why the rest of the squadron was beating his score until he figured out what Antilles had done. Following the exercise, the squadron gathered in [[DownTime]], the base's [[Cantina/Legends|cantina]], where most of the members considered presenting a formal protest over Antilles' actions towards Horn to [[General/Legends|General]] [[Horton Salm/Legends|Horton Salm]], the base commander. When Horn arrived after confronting Antilles over the commander's actions, Vri'syk was apathetic to his presence while the rest of the squadron were pleased to see him. Later during their training, Vri'syk and the squadron participated in a simulated attack on [[Defender Wing]], a unit of three [[BTL Y-wing starfighter/Legends|BTL Y-wing starfighter]] training squadrons led by General Salm. The assault ended with the Rogues registering thirty simulated kills for the loss of four of their own.<ref name="X-Wing: Rogue Squadron" />

A [[Standard month/Legends|month]] after the Rogue Squadron roster was finalized, the unit was declared operational. Antilles believed that the unit was still green, however. He had expected between four-to-six months of advanced training before taking the squadron into combat, but he could not deny that his pilots were superior fliers and that their training scores were better than those of operational line units. As part of the New Republic's [[Core Campaign|campaign]] to advance into the [[Core Worlds/Legends|Core Worlds]] in an attempt to liberate the [[The galaxy/Legends|galactic]] [[Capital/Legends|capital]] of [[Coruscant/Legends|Coruscant]], Rogue Squadron was moved to the planet [[Talasea]] in the [[Morobe system]]. During the transfer, the Rogues were pulled out of [[Hyperspace/Legends|hyperspace]] in the [[Chorax system/Legends|Chorax system]] by the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Imperial]] [[Immobilizer 418 Interdictor-class Heavy Cruiser|interdictor cruiser]] [[Corusca Rainbow|''Black Asp'']], which was attempting to apprehend the [[Smuggler/Legends|smuggler]] [[Mirax Terrik Horn|Mirax Terrik]] and her [[Starship/Legends|ship]], the ''[[Pulsar Skate]]''. The Rogues [[Battle in the Chorax system|engaged]] the Imperial ship and its squadron of [[TIE/ln space superiority starfighter/Legends|TIE starfighters]], driving them off with no losses to the Rogues.<ref name="X-Wing: Rogue Squadron" />

Vri'syk and his squadronmates' first official action against the Empire was at the [[Rout of Hensara]], a mission to protect the [[Battle of Yavin (starship)|''Battle of Yavin'']], a modified [[Imperial Customs Frigate|customs frigate]]. Assigned to Coreward [[Scout/Legends|scouting]] missions, the ''Battle of Yavin'' had encountered the [[Strike-class medium cruiser|''Strike''-class medium cruiser]] [[Havoc (Strike-class)|''Havoc'']] in the [[Hensara system/Legends|Hensara system]]. Forced to go to ground to avoid capture, the ship's crew sank the vessel in a deep [[Water/Legends|lake]] on the planet [[Hensara III]]. The ''Havoc'' had landed ground forces to search for the ship and crew, then left the system, giving the New Republic a chance to effect a rescue mission. During the operation, the ''Havoc'' returned, having been retrofitted with starfighter [[Hangar/Legends|hangars]] and carrying thirty-six [[TIE series/Legends|TIE Series]] craft—six [[TIE/IN interceptor/Legends|TIE interceptors]], six [[TIE/sa tactical bomber/Legends|TIE bombers]], and twenty-four TIE starfighters. The Rogues engaged the Imperials, and Vri'syk [[Death/Legends|killed]] at least two enemy fighters. The New Republic won the battle, destroying thirty-four craft with no losses. On Talasea after the battle, [[Rogue Five/Legends|Rogue Five]], [[Gavin Darklighter]], who secured no kills during the engagement, was jokingly named "worst pilot in the squadron" rather than the Bothan, a fact that Vri'syk was visibly relieved about.<ref name="X-Wing: Rogue Squadron" />

One night on Talasea, a [[Platoon/Legends|platoon]] of Imperial [[Stormtrooper/Legends|stormtroopers]] was dispatched to the base by Admiral [[Devlia]], commander of Imperial forces in the [[Rachuk sector/Legends|Rachuk sector]], based on information provided by [[Imperial Intelligence/Legends|Imperial Intelligence]] agent [[Kirtan Loor]] who was actively hunting the Rogues. Vri'syk survived the covert attack, which claimed the life of Rogue pilot [[Lujayne Forge]] and left his squadronmates Horn, Darklighter and [[Andoorni Hui]] seriously injured. Vri'syk attended Forge's funeral and, later, participated in a [[Defense of Vladet|retaliatory strike]] against Devlia and his forces at [[Grand Isle]], their base on the planet [[Vladet]]. Once again, the battle proved to be a victory for the New Republic with no losses to Rogue Squadron.<ref name="X-Wing: Rogue Squadron" />

===Death===
{{Quote|Damn, Peshk caught that one. He's gone.|Corran Horn's thoughts|<ref name="X-Wing: Rogue Squadron" />}}
[[File:RogueSquadron_cover_art.jpg|thumb|right|150px|The First Battle of Borleias, where Peshk Vri'syk was killed.]]
As part of the New Republic's offensive towards the Core, a [[New Republic task force (Borleias)|task force]] was assigned to capture the planet [[Borleias/Legends|Borleias]], a target codenamed "[[Blackmoon]]." The New Republic intended to use Borleias as a staging point for the assault on Coruscant. The mission was commanded by the Bothan General, [[Laryn Kre'fey]], and the [[Bothan Spynet/Legends|Bothan Spynet]] indicated that Borleias was poorly defended. Rogue Squadron was assigned to the [[Task force/Legends|task force]], charged with escorting [[Assault shuttle/Legends|assault shuttles]] to the surface once the larger [[Capital ship/Legends|capital ships]] had breached the [[Planetary shield/Legends|planetary shield]] and subdued its defenses. However, the Imperial commander, General [[Evir Derricote]], had extra defenses hidden on the planet of which the Bothans were unaware. During the [[First Battle of Borleias|engagement]], Derricote allowed the New Republic to believe that they were weakening his shields with their assault by gradually reduced power to them, lulling them into a false sense of security. Once the shields had dropped and no defensive fire came from the surface, General Kre'fey ordered General Salm's Defender Wing away from the engagement—the unit had originally been slated to sweep the planet's defenses with a [[Bomber/Legends|bombing]] run, but Derricote's actions had convinced the Bothan General that such an action was unnecessary—and ordered the Rogues to escort the shuttles in.<ref name="X-Wing: Rogue Squadron" />

Vri'syk and his squadronmates formed up on the shuttles, escorting them to their target. As they approached, Derricote sprung his trap, re-establishing his planetary shield and firing surface based [[Ion cannon/Legends|ion cannons]] at the task force. The first barrage of ion fire struck the ''[[Modaran]]'', Kre'fey's command shuttle, and sent it slamming into the shield. Vri'syk's starfighter, positioned between one of the other shuttles and the shield, was struck by another ion blast, which vaporised him and his craft. The New Republic task force effected a retreat from the battle, while the Rogues also lost Andoorni Hui to enemy fire.<ref name="X-Wing: Rogue Squadron" />

Despite Vri'syk's brief career with Rogue Squadron, the Bothans exaggerated his accomplishment and hailed him as a hero in order to advance their political achievements, comparing him to the [[Martyrs]] who secured the second Death Star plans.<ref name="The Krytos Trap">''[[X-Wing: The Krytos Trap]]''</ref> More than [[9 ABY/Legends|three years later]], Wedge Antilles still regretted the loss of Vri'syk, and would remember him while recalling other close friends who had perished in battle, including [[Biggs Darklighter/Legends|Biggs Darklighter]], [[Jek Tono Porkins/Legends|Jek Porkins]], [[Dak Ralter/Legends|Dak Ralter]] and others.<ref name="Isard's Revenge">''[[X-Wing: Isard's Revenge]]''</ref>

==Personality and traits==
{{Quote|You are well on your way to taking your place in the constellations of Bothan heroes like the Martyrs and even your predecessor in Rogue Squadron, Peshk Vri'syk.|Borsk Fey'lya, to Asyr Sei'lar|<ref name="The Krytos Trap" />}}
Peshk Vri'syk was a [[Color/Legends|ruddy]]-[[Hair/Legends|furred]] Bothan who was considered to be an excellent pilot. While not as impressive as fellow Rogues Corran Horn or Bror Jace,<ref name="X-Wing: Rogue Squadron" /> Vri'syk stood out among his classmates at the Bothan Martial Academy.<ref name="Wedge's Gamble" /> Vri'syk possessed a great amount of pride in his abilities; while his colleagues felt guilty for excelling in a Folor training run at Horn's expense, Vri'syk took great delight in bragging about his score. He was also relieved to not be regarded as the worst pilot in the squadron following the Battle of Hensara, a distinction that fell on Gavin Darklighter. Vri'syk was also the most socially detached of the Rogues. He seldom engaged in conversation with his fellow pilots, and he reacted apathetically to Horn after the Folor training session, while the other pilots were happy to see him and apologetic for benefiting at his expense by the use of his targeting data.<ref name="X-Wing: Rogue Squadron" />

Vri'syk's given name, Peshk, meant "handsome" in [[Bothese]]. His family name, Vri, translated as either "groundskeeper" or "caretaker". The "'syk" honorific suffix indicated that he belonged to either [[Clan Asyk]], or [[Clan Isyk]].<ref name="Galactic">''[[Galactic Campaign Guide]]''</ref>

==Behind the scenes==
Peshk Vri'syk was created by [[Michael A. Stackpole]] and first appeared in ''[[X-Wing: Rogue Squadron]]'', the first novel in the [[Star Wars: X-Wing (novel series)|''X-Wing'' series]] published in [[1996]]. He was first illustrated in the [[Wikipedia:Japan|Japanese]] language edition of the ''Rogue Squadron'' novel. Vri'syk garnered mentions in other entries in the X-wing series, including ''[[X-Wing: Wedge's Gamble]]'', ''[[X-Wing: The Krytos Trap]]''—both also published in 1996—and ''[[X-Wing: Isard's Revenge]]'', which was released in [[1999]]. He later received entries in both the ''[[Star Wars Encyclopedia]]'' in [[1998]] and [[2008]]'s ''[[The Complete Star Wars Encyclopedia]]'', and was mentioned in [[The Official Star Wars Fact File 14|the fourteenth issue]] of ''[[The Official Star Wars Fact File]]''.
